commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e94ac92c838977f6d009a417666a703d5ff3e4de)
commands.review_summary:
Adds external-action evidence metadata for tools and enforces SMS-style receipt-claim guards across agent streaming, block reply, terminal payload, retry, and plugin registry paths.

PR surface: Source +570, Tests +726. Total +1296 across 25 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the PR enforces a global receipt guard while current main's built-in SMS message path returns receipt payloads without plugin metadata evidence. I did not run a live SMS send in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer or contributor revision because the code defects are tied to missing real behavior proof and a new plugin API decision.

target.title: fix(agents): require tool evidence for external action receipts
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a generic external-action evidence contract for tools that perform side effects outside OpenClaw, such as sending a message, booking something, creating a ticket, or triggering a provider API action.
- Records successful current-run action evidence and carries it through retries, terminal payload construction, partial replies, block replies, and chunked block delivery.
- Gates user-visible delivery/receipt claims on matching current-run evidence, so an agent cannot confidently report an external side effect as completed unless the runtime has evidence from the tool result.
- Includes SMS as the first concrete receipt-claim detector because that is the current reproducible case, but the API shape is intentionally provider- and action-family-neutral.

## Problem
Agents can produce user-visible confirmations for external side effects that look authoritative even when the current run has no verified tool evidence for that action. This is not specific to one provider. The unsafe pattern is:

1. A tool call may fail, be skipped, be dry-run, be retried, or return a result that is not tied to the final assistant text.
2. The assistant can still say something like “sent”, “queued”, “accepted”, or include a provider-looking message ID.
3. Downstream users see a confident receipt and reasonably assume the external action happened.

For upstream OpenClaw, the invariant should be: user-visible receipt claims for externally visible side effects need evidence from the same run, owned by the tool or plugin that performed the action.

## What changed
- Plugin tool metadata can declare how to extract external action evidence from successful tool results.
- The agent runtime records that evidence only for the concrete plugin-owned tool that executed, not for same-named tools from another plugin.
- Recorded evidence is accumulated across retry/failover attempts in the same run.
- Assistant output paths consult the same evidence before delivering receipt-like claims:
  - partial streaming replies
  - streamed block replies
  - chunked block replies
  - terminal payloads
- The first concrete claim detector covers SMS-style delivery receipts because that is the known failing shape and because SMS receipts are common in channel/plugin surfaces.

## Linked context
- No exact existing issue or PR found for external action receipt evidence before opening this PR.
- Related but not duplicate: #90998 covers SMS text command auth, not side-effect receipt evidence.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: an agent could make a user-visible external-action receipt claim with provider-like fields even when the current run had no matching successful tool-result evidence.
- Concrete reproducible case used for this PR: SMS-style delivery receipt text with `Status: accepted/queued` and `Message ID`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto upstream `origin/main` at `3c9cf2d5833d106713c640f806a1eb35827f3e0c`, commit `e94ac92c838977f6d009a417666a703d5ff3e4de`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/external-action-receipts.test.ts \
  src/agents/external-action-receipt-claims.test.ts \
  src/agents/external-action-receipt-guard.test.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-block-replies-text-end-does-not.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  83 passed (83)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  64 passed (64)
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  81 passed (81)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 15.60s

unsupported= {"allowed":false,"claim":{"actionFamily":"sms","recipient":"+13522815065","sender":"+14155201316","providerId":"4797682962735104","status":"accepted/queued"},"replacementText":"I cannot verify that this SMS was sent. I do not have matching current-turn delivery evidence, so please check the messaging provider history or use the verified send flow before reporting it as sent."}
verified= {"allowed":true}
nonSms= {"allowed":true}
```

- Observed result after fix: unsupported receipt text is replaced before terminal payload delivery, partial streaming, streamed block replies, and chunked block replies. Matching current-run evidence allows the receipt. Non-SMS receipts are not classified as SMS by the first detector.
- What was not tested: a live provider send against production credentials was not performed in upstream proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof currently uses synthetic tool-result evidence shaped like provider output. ClawSweeper correctly asked for proof against the built-in message-tool send path before merge.
- Before evidence: the motivating regression was observed in a live operator workflow where the external provider UI/history did not show the claimed sent message even though the agent reported a sent/queued receipt.

## Tests and validation
- `timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/external-action-receipts.test.ts src/agents/external-action-receipt-claims.test.ts src/agents/external-action-receipt-guard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-block-replies-text-end-does-not.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
- `timeout -k5s 120s ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/external-action-receipt-claims.ts src/agents/external-action-receipt-guard.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts`
- `timeout -k5s 120s ./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/external-action-receipt-claims.ts src/agents/external-action-receipt-guard.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD -- .`
- `timeout -k5s 900s codex review --base origin/main`: final review reported no discrete, actionable regressions before the final upstream rebase. Rebase onto latest upstream was clean and focused tests were rerun afterward.

## Current review state
ClawSweeper found one important upstream-readiness gap after PR creation:

- The built-in `message` tool SMS path can return receipt data but does not currently feed this new evidence accumulator.
- That means the guard could block truthful built-in message-tool receipt confirmations unless the built-in path is normalized into the same evidence model.
- Real behavior proof from an actual send path is still needed before this should merge.

That finding is valid. The next revision should either record built-in message-tool send receipts into this evidence model or narrow enforcement to surfaces that can produce evidence.

## Risk checklist
- [x] Plugin metadata is owner-scoped by plugin ID plus tool name, so another plugin cannot spoof evidence metadata for a same-named tool.
- [x] Dry-run and failed results are not recorded as successful evidence.
- [x] Current-run evidence is accumulated across retry attempts.
- [x] Streamed partial replies, block replies, chunked block replies, and terminal payloads enforce the same receipt invariant.
- [x] Reply routing metadata is preserved when replacing unsupported receipt text.
- [x] Non-SMS receipts and explanatory SMS documentation are not treated as sent SMS receipts.
- [ ] Built-in message-tool send receipts still need to feed the evidence accumulator.
- [ ] Real provider/tool proof still needs to be added.

## Badge
[size/exempt: safety invariant spans plugin metadata contract, evidence collection, retry accumulation, streamed replies, chunked block replies, terminal payloads, and regression tests; splitting would leave a non-enforcing partial change.]

## AI Assistance
This PR was authored with Codex. Local Codex review was run and actionable findings were addressed. Testing depth is listed above.
target.author: kesslerio

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
